Package coxprogramming.processing.sprites

Class CompoundSprite

Object
Sprite
CompoundSprite
Direct Known Subclasses:
AnimatedSprite
public class CompoundSprite extends Sprite
A sprite made up of multiple other sprites that move together as a group.

A CompoundSprite acts as a container. You add child sprites to it, and from then on they all move in concert whenever the compound moves. Each child can still have its own independent velocity on top of the group velocity â€” for example, an orbiting moon that also drifts across the screen, or eyes on a character that blink while the character walks.

CompoundSprite uses a builder pattern so you can set it up in one readable chain of calls: 

   CompoundSprite player = new CompoundSprite(this, 200, 300)
       .addSprite(body)
       .addSprite(head)
       .addSprite(leftArm)
       .addSprite(rightArm);

   player.setVelocity(3, 0); // the whole character walks right

How velocity works

When you call setVelocity(float, float) on the compound, the change in velocity is applied to every child so they all gain (or lose) exactly the same velocity shift. This means a child's independent velocity is always preserved: 

   // Give a child its own upward drift before adding it
   moon.setVelocity(0, -1);        // moon drifts up on its own
   system.addSprite(moon);
   system.setVelocity(2, 0);       // whole system moves right
   // moon now has vel (2, -1): group velocity + its own drift

Positions

Each child's position is absolute (in screen pixels). When you create a compound sprite, place the children at their desired starting positions before calling addSprite(coxprogramming.processing.sprites.Sprite). Methods that translate the whole group (move(), moveTo(float, float), followMouse()) will keep all children in formation.

    Fields inherited from class Sprite

    parent, pos, screenBehavior, vel

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    CompoundSprite(PApplet parent, float x, float y)
    Creates a CompoundSprite at position (x, y) with no initial velocity.
    CompoundSprite(PApplet parent, float x, float y, float dx, float dy)
    Creates a CompoundSprite at position (x, y) with a given starting velocity.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    CompoundSprite
    addSprite(Sprite s)
    Adds a child sprite to this compound and returns this so calls can be chained.
    void
    drawSprite()
    Draws every child sprite in the order they were added.
    void
    followMouse()
    Moves the whole group toward the mouse cursor at the current speed, keeping all children in formation.
    ArrayList<Sprite>
    getSprites()
    Returns the list of child sprites belonging to this compound.
    void
    keyboardControl()
    Reads arrow-key input and updates the group velocity accordingly, propagating the velocity change to every child.
    void
    move()
    Moves the compound sprite forward one step by its velocity, wraps it around the screen edges if needed, then calls move() on every child.
    void
    moveTo(float x, float y)
    Instantly moves the whole group to (x, y).
    void
    moveTo(Vector2D newPosition)
    Instantly moves the whole group to the given position vector.
    CompoundSprite
    removeSprite(Sprite s)
    Removes a child sprite from this compound and returns this so calls can be chained.
    void
    setVelocity(float dx, float dy)
    Sets the group velocity and applies the velocity change to every child sprite, so each child gains or loses the same amount.
    void
    setVelocity(Vector2D newVelocity)
    Sets the group velocity from a Vector2D and applies the change to every child sprite.

    distanceTo, getPosition, getVelocity

  • Constructor Details

    • CompoundSprite

      public CompoundSprite(PApplet parent, float x, float y)
      Creates a CompoundSprite at position (x, y) with no initial velocity.
      Parameters:
      parent - your Processing sketch â€” pass this
      x - the starting x position of the compound's anchor point
      y - the starting y position of the compound's anchor point

    • CompoundSprite

      public CompoundSprite(PApplet parent, float x, float y, float dx, float dy)
      Creates a CompoundSprite at position (x, y) with a given starting velocity. The velocity is not automatically applied to children until you call addSprite(coxprogramming.processing.sprites.Sprite) â€” children added later will receive the current velocity.
      Parameters:
      parent - your Processing sketch â€” pass this
      x - the starting x position
      y - the starting y position
      dx - horizontal velocity in pixels per frame
      dy - vertical velocity in pixels per frame

  • Method Details

    • addSprite

      public CompoundSprite addSprite(Sprite s)
      Adds a child sprite to this compound and returns this so calls can be chained. The compound's current group velocity is immediately added to the child's velocity so it moves in sync from the first frame.
      Parameters:
      s - the sprite to add
      Returns:
      this CompoundSprite, for chaining

    • removeSprite

      public CompoundSprite removeSprite(Sprite s)
      Removes a child sprite from this compound and returns this so calls can be chained. The compound's current group velocity is subtracted from the child's velocity, restoring its original independent motion.
      Parameters:
      s - the sprite to remove
      Returns:
      this CompoundSprite, for chaining

    • getSprites

      public ArrayList<Sprite> getSprites()
      Returns the list of child sprites belonging to this compound. You can use this to access individual children and adjust their independent velocities.
      Returns:
      the list of child sprites

    • setVelocity

      public void setVelocity(float dx, float dy)
      Sets the group velocity and applies the velocity change to every child sprite, so each child gains or loses the same amount. A child's own independent velocity is preserved on top of the group velocity.

      For example, if a child has velocity (1, -2) and the group velocity changes from (0, 0) to (3, 0), the child will end up with (4, -2).

      Overrides:
      setVelocity in class Sprite
      Parameters:
      dx - the new horizontal group velocity
      dy - the new vertical group velocity

    • setVelocity

      public void setVelocity(Vector2D newVelocity)
      Sets the group velocity from a Vector2D and applies the change to every child sprite. See setVelocity(float, float) for a full explanation.
      Overrides:
      setVelocity in class Sprite
      Parameters:
      newVelocity - the new group velocity

    • move

      public void move()
      Moves the compound sprite forward one step by its velocity, wraps it around the screen edges if needed, then calls move() on every child. Because each child already carries the group velocity as part of its own velocity (added when setVelocity(float, float) was called), all children automatically stay in formation.
      Overrides:
      move in class Sprite

    • moveTo

      public void moveTo(float x, float y)
      Instantly moves the whole group to (x, y). The compound jumps to the new position and every child is translated by the same offset, keeping all relative positions intact.
      Overrides:
      moveTo in class Sprite
      Parameters:
      x - the new x position for the compound's anchor
      y - the new y position for the compound's anchor

    • moveTo

      public void moveTo(Vector2D newPosition)
      Instantly moves the whole group to the given position vector. See moveTo(float, float) for details.
      Overrides:
      moveTo in class Sprite
      Parameters:
      newPosition - the new anchor position

    • followMouse

      public void followMouse()
      Moves the whole group toward the mouse cursor at the current speed, keeping all children in formation. If the group's speed is 0, nothing moves â€” set a velocity first with setVelocity(float, float).
      Overrides:
      followMouse in class Sprite

    • keyboardControl

      public void keyboardControl()
      Reads arrow-key input and updates the group velocity accordingly, propagating the velocity change to every child. Call this inside your sketch's keyPressed() function.
      Overrides:
      keyboardControl in class Sprite

    • drawSprite

      public void drawSprite()
      Draws every child sprite in the order they were added. The compound itself has no visual appearance â€” it is only a container. Call this once per frame inside your draw() loop.
      Specified by:
      drawSprite in class Sprite
